ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pizza class (FOODON_00002475); no specific stuffed-crust pizza subclass exists. Mapped to the nearest broader class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00KY covers pizza as a category. No stuffed-crust-specific code exists; broader match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for stuffed crust pizza or pizza as a food item in the clinical terminology scope.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one slice of a standard 12-inch stuffed crust pizza, weighing approximately 107 g, providing around 257 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, cheese, frozen, baked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21299); cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database entry for stuffed crust pizza (F05020108)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary macro values (energy 240 kcal, protein 12.85 g, fat 9.8 g, carbohydrate 24.98 g, sugar 1.55 g, sodium 532 mg, dietary fibre 1.45 g) are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo record F05020108 at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central cheese pizza data (NDB 21299, ~4.0 g/100 g) as HPB record does not report saturated fat separately. Micronutrient fields not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for stuffed crust pizza specifically. GI of 60 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for pizza (cheese pizza, white flour base, GI ~60, medium category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (60) × available carbohydrate per serving (26.7 g for 107 g serving) / 100 = 16.0 GL units.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content at 532 mg per 100 g, contributed by processed cheese, cured meat toppings, and tomato sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit stuffed crust pizza to occasional consumption and request reduced-sodium toppings, as a single 107 g slice delivers approximately 569 mg sodium — nearly 25% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined white flour crust with 24.98 g carbohydrate per 100 g and low dietary fibre (1.45 g/100 g), yielding a medium-to-high glycemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should restrict portion size to one slice, pair with a high-fibre salad to blunt postprandial glucose rise, and preferably request a wholemeal or cauliflower crust alternative to reduce the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat of approximately 4.0 g per 100 g from cheese and processed meat toppings, combined with high sodium (532 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, stuffed crust pizza should be consumed sparingly due to its combined saturated fat and sodium load; choosing vegetable-only toppings, reduced-fat cheese, and a thin crust can meaningfully lower the atherogenic and hypertensive risk per serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory load from processed meat toppings (saturated fat, sodium nitrates), refined flour, and nightshade-based tomato sauce
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid stuffed crust pizza with processed meat toppings, as the combination of saturated fat, sodium nitrates, and refined carbohydrates amplifies systemic inflammatory cytokine activity; vegetable-only toppings and a wholemeal base are preferable alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stuffed crust pizza is a Western-style baked pizza distinguished by a hollow outer crust that is filled with melted cheese — typically mozzarella or a processed cheese blend — before baking. The base is made from refined white wheat flour dough, topped with tomato sauce, shredded mozzarella, and a variety of toppings such as pepperoni, salami, bell peppers, mushrooms, or olives. The crust is sealed around the cheese filling and baked in a deck or conveyor oven at high heat, producing a crisp exterior and a soft, cheese-oozing inner rim. The flavour profile is rich, savoury, and umami-forward, with the cheese-stuffed crust adding a creamy, indulgent contrast to the tomato-forward topping layer. Nutritionally, each 100 g delivers 240 kcal, 12.85 g protein, 9.8 g fat, and 24.98 g carbohydrate with only 1.45 g dietary fibre, reflecting a refined-flour, high-sodium (532 mg/100 g) profile. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 60 (medium), making portion control important for blood glucose management. Clinically, the high sodium content raises concern for hypertension, the refined carbohydrate load is problematic for type 2 diabetes management, and the saturated fat from cheese and processed meats is relevant for cardiovascular disease risk. Stuffed crust pizza is widely consumed as a casual dining or takeaway meal in Singapore and globally, often shared in social or family settings.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of stuffed crust pizza?
Can diabetics eat stuffed crust pizza?
Is stuffed crust pizza high in sodium?
What is the GI of pizza?
Is stuffed crust pizza suitable for hypertension?
How much protein is in stuffed crust pizza?
Is stuffed crust pizza high in saturated fat?
Can I eat pizza if I have high blood pressure?
What are healthier pizza crust alternatives?

confidence_reason: Core macro values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database record F05020108 at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from the closest USDA FoodData Central cheese pizza entry (NDB 21299). GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous cheese pizza entry. All primary nutrient values are from approved sources.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient, and moisture data for stuffed crust pizza are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database record F05020108, and no exact match exists in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. Saturated fat of 4.0 g/100 g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 21299 (cheese pizza, frozen, baked) as the closest approved-source equivalent. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database cheese pizza data; no direct measurement for stuffed crust pizza was found. The input record listed occasion_tag 'breakfast' which is atypical for pizza; this has been retained in meal_context as 'casual dining' and 'dinner' reflecting more typical consumption patterns. No zero-value core macros are present in this record.
